title: Conductor craft ratio
lede: Move the baton between volume and taste. AI can flood the room with takes. The conductor decides how much craft survives the flood.

anchors.label: 90% machine throughput
anchors.throughput: Maximum generation. More shots, drafts, alternates, and tests arrive before your coffee gets cold.
anchors.craft: The human work shifts to triage: naming the brief, rejecting cheap-looking frames, and refusing to confuse abundance with finish.
anchors.cost: Costs collapse toward the Kevin Friel receipt: six-figure video energy pushed toward a $200, 2.5-day experiment.
anchors.loss: The loss is sameness. If the prompt is vague or the selector is tired, the pile gets bigger while the point gets thinner.
anchors.sourceNote: Kevin's line: $100,000 budgets to $200 in 2.5 days, same quality, real project.

anchors.label: 50 / 50 studio
anchors.throughput: The stack does the heavy lift: Midjourney frames, Runway motion, ElevenLabs audio, ComfyUI glue, Topaz polish.
anchors.craft: The human stays in the loop for taste, timing, continuity, disclosure, and the final no. Tools get parts; you keep direction.
anchors.cost: Budget and schedule still drop hard, but the savings fund iteration instead of replacing judgment.
anchors.loss: The risk is workflow theatre: fancy nodes that break momentum, or too many passes that sand the idea flat.
anchors.sourceNote: Kevin frames the work as chain thinking, not one app and pray.

anchors.label: 10% machine assist
anchors.throughput: Fewer outputs arrive, but each one carries more direct human intention and context.
anchors.craft: Craft is high-touch: story, shot choice, edit rhythm, texture, and meaning are argued by people before tools are asked to help.
anchors.cost: The budget climbs because human time returns to the center. Use this when the relationship, material, or risk demands it.
anchors.loss: The loss is access. Some people and projects stay locked out when every step requires old-world time, gear, or specialist labor.
anchors.sourceNote: Kevin's access argument: the barrier to entry disappeared; now ideas matter more than equipment.

closing.title: The slider is not moral. The conductor is.
closing.body: A 90% machine pass can be generous when it gives access. A 10% machine pass can be necessary when the material needs hands. The craft is knowing which room you are in.
